descriptions.monitors_sensor-checks: List the sensor-check monitors defined across the Automate fleet (GET /SensorChecks, no parameters). Returns an array of Monitor records with Id, Name, MonitorType, ComputerId/ComputerName, Status, LastScan, AlertCount, and FailureCount. Use this for the configured sensor-style monitors specifically; use monitors_list (GET /MonitorStatistics) when you want every monitor with its alerting statistics.
descriptions.patching_approval-policies: List the patch approval policies configured in Automate (GET /ApprovalPolicies, no parameters). Returns an array of PatchPolicy records with Id, Name, Description, and IsEnabled. Use this to see which approval policies exist and whether each is enabled before deploying patches; pair with patching_microsoft-policies and patching_thirdparty-policies for the full patch-policy picture.
descriptions.patching_microsoft-policies: List the Microsoft update policies configured in Automate (GET /MicrosoftUpdatePolicies, no parameters). Returns an array of PatchPolicy records with Id, Name, Description, and IsEnabled. Use this when you specifically need the Microsoft/Windows update rules, as distinct from third-party (patching_thirdparty-policies) or general approval (patching_approval-policies) policies.
descriptions.patching_thirdparty-policies: List the third-party update policies configured in Automate (GET /ThirdPartyUpdatePolicies, no parameters). Returns an array of PatchPolicy records with Id, Name, Description, and IsEnabled. Use this for non-Microsoft application patch rules, as distinct from Microsoft (patching_microsoft-policies) or general approval (patching_approval-policies) policies.
descriptions.server_db-time: Get the current time reported by the Automate database server (GET /DatabaseServerTime, no parameters). Returns a ServerTime object with a single Time field (date-time). Use this to detect clock skew or to anchor relative time windows (e.g. since/stale-agent calculations) against the server's own clock rather than the local machine's.
descriptions.server_info: Get the Automate server's version and identifying metadata (GET /System/ServerInformation, no parameters). Returns a ServerInformation object with Version, ServerName, and DatabaseVersion. Use this for a lightweight reachability and version check (it backs the doctor/health commands); prefer server_db-time when you specifically need the server clock rather than version info.
